entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud - Ineligible ITC (NVV) Separate G/L Posting and GST Reporting
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I'm working on SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (India GST) and need some guidance regarding Ineligible ITC.

Currently, SAP standard uses the NVV account key for non-deductible tax, where the GST amount is absorbed into the expense/asset and no separate Input GST G/L line is created.

My client has requested the following:

- Create separate G/Ls for Ineligible IGST, CGST, and SGST.

- Post Ineligible ITC to these G/Ls instead of absorbing it into the expense.

- Show the amount under GSTR-3B Table 4(B)(1).

My concern is:

-If I replace NVV with input tax account keys (such as JII/JIC/JIS) mapped to new Ineligible ITC G/Ls, will SAP still treat the tax as Non-Deductible ITC, or will it be considered eligible ITC?

-Will this impact other GST reports such as the Purchase Register, GST Registers, or DRC reports?

-Is there a standard SAP-supported approach in S/4HANA Public Cloud to achieve separate G/L posting for Ineligible ITC while preserving the correct statutory GST reporting?

Has anyone implemented a similar requirement? Any guidance or best practices would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Import button not showing in the display dimension APP
entry.content:
hello, 

For details on replication of unit of measures and dimensions, refer Code List Replication.

The Import option is only available if at least one unit of measure was previously created through file import. If all units of measure were created through data replication, the import option is disabled. You need not perform import dimensions or import unit of measures.

Regards,
Pravesh

Additionally, the client strictly prohibits implementing any new SAP Notes on the S/4HANA side due to concerns about impacting other existing processes. Standard Export Job from SAC to S/4HANA via API_FINANCIALPLANDATA_SRV: My main concern here is that this standard API always executes in "Replacement Mode" (reversing the existing scope and reposting). Over time, this will definitely cause the ACDOCP table to bloat with reversal line items. Is there any known workaround to make this standard export function in a true "Delta" mode for S/4HANA 1909? 2. entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public WM – How to group multiple p...
entry.content:
Good day @Gabriel_Salomez 

Thank you for your question.

Based on your scenario, the Public Cloud does not merge warehouse tasks with different source storage bins into one warehouse task. Each source bin movement must remain separately traceable.

However, the standard solution is for you to group the warehouse tasks into one warehouse order. The warehouse order acts as one work package for the operator, while the individual warehouse tasks remain visible and are confirmed separately. See Warehouse Order Creation for more details.

So, in simple terms:

  • You cannot turn ten different source bin movements into one warehouse task.
  • You can group the ten tasks into one warehouse order.
  • You can sort the tasks according to the preferred warehouse travel sequence.
  • The operator can process the warehouse order through RF or using one printed pick list.
  • You can use a pick handling unit to collect the products during the same warehouse trip.

I recommend you first check whether the ten warehouse tasks are already assigned to one warehouse order.

Open Process Warehouse Tasks-Picking,  

filter for the ten affected warehouse tasks, open the task details and check the following values:

  • Warehouse order number
  • Source storage bin
  • Destination storage bin
  • Warehouse task status

All ten warehouse tasks should have the same warehouse order number where the configured warehouse order creation rule and limit permit this.

If different warehouse order numbers are displayed, ask the warehouse config expert to review the following configuration acitivities in the configuration environment:

  1. Define how the tasks should be ordered for warehouse execution.
  2. Assign the available sort fields required for the warehouse travel sequence.
  3. Check that the maximum number of tasks and any weight, volume, or processing limits do not split the ten tasks into separate warehouse orders.
  4. Confirm that the correct sort rule, filter and limit values are included in the applicable creation rule.
  5. Confirm that the rule you assigned to the activity area are used by the affected source bins.

Those steps are provided in the link below.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/e12ec3b67c6a4ad694904419e4edae37.html?locale=en-US

After you are done with checking the configuration, create a fresh test set of ten warehouse task. The task should remain separate, but the system should assign them to one warehouse order and sorts them according to the sequence you configured.

The operator can then process the warehouse order through the RF picking transaction.

The printed Pick List can also show the warehouse order, individual tasks, source bins, destination bins, products and quantities.

Combined Picking is an additional option, but it only combines RF picking tasks with matching attributes. It should not be treated as a general method for merging tasks from different source bins.

Waves can coordinate and release warehouse activities together, but they do not replace warehouse order creation or merge the physical movements. See Processing of Waves for mor details.

In summary:

The tasks cannot be merged into one warehouse task because the source bins are different. The recommended standard approach is for you to group and sort them within one warehouse order and execute the order through RF or a pick list. If the tasks are still split, review the creation rule, activity area assignment, sort rule, and active warehouse order limits.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Planning: Where AI meets strategy and execution
entry.content:
SAP Sapphire introduced a new vision for Enterprise Planning, and it marks an important step forward for organizations looking to connect strategy with execution through AI.

If you work with SAP Analytics Cloud for Planning or SAP Business Data Cloud, this webinar will help you understand where these solutions are heading and why that matters for your customers. You'll see how AI agents, trusted business data, and managed business content come together to simplify planning, accelerate decision making, and create a more connected planning experience.

The thing is, planning has become much more than creating budgets or forecasts. It now means responding faster to change while keeping finance, operations, and strategy aligned.

Join us to explore SAP's roadmap for Enterprise Planning, discover the innovations announced at SAP Sapphire, and learn how today's SAP SAC and SAP Business Data Cloud investments are shaping the next generation of intelligent planning.

Register now and be among the first to explore what's next. https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/jun/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00014536.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPEcosystem #BusinessAI #SAPAnalyticsCloud #BusinessDataCloud #EnterprisePlanning

entry.title: 从 Assistant 到 Agent：理解 SAP 新一代代理式 AI 创新
entry.content:
AP Sapphire 2026 进一步明确了 SAP Business AI 的演进方向：AI 不再只是嵌入在单个应用中的辅助功能，而是逐步走向面向业务流程的 Assistant 与 Agent 协同模式。

在这一模式下，用户可以通过自然语言表达业务目标，系统基于角色、流程上下文和可信业务数据，协调不同的 AI Agent 执行任务，并在必要时触发确定性的系统动作。SAP Cloud ERP 的智能能力也因此从“单点辅助”进一步演进为更具情境感知、任务关联和业务导向性的代理式 AI 能力。

本文将围绕 SAP 新一代 Assistant、Agent 与 Skill 的概念展开，并结合采购寻源场景，帮助客户和合作伙伴理解这一能力如何支持 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 中的业务流程创新。

Assistant、Agent 与 Skill 的关系

在 SAP 新一代 AI 体验中，Assistant、Agent 和 Skill 分别承担不同职责。

Assistant 是面向业务用户的入口。
它利用用户角色和流程上下文，理解用户提出的业务意图，并协调相关 Agent 执行跨业务的复杂工作流。Assistant 更像一位智能团队成员，帮助用户把业务目标拆解为可以执行的任务。

Agent 是面向特定目标构建的 AI 代理。
它具备深厚的业务流程专业知识，可通过 Assistant 访问，并自动化处理定义明确的多步骤企业工作流。Agent 的重点不是回答问题，而是围绕具体业务目标执行任务、分析信息并提出行动建议。

Skill 则是更专业化、原子化和确定性的动作能力。
例如检索业务数据、调用规则、触发系统事务或执行预定义步骤。Skill 使 Agent 能够在安全、可控的边界内完成具体动作。

简单来说：

  • Assistant 负责理解意图和协调工作
  • Agent 负责完成面向业务目标的任务
  • Skill 负责执行确定性的系统动作

这也是 SAP 代理式 AI 与通用聊天式 AI 的重要区别：它不是简单地生成文本，而是面向企业业务流程完成工作。

示例：寻源助手如何协调多个 Agent

 

以上图中的采购寻源场景为例，用户不再需要分别进入多个应用，手工查找供应商、准备 RFx、比较投标并整理谈判策略，而是可以通过一个“寻源助手”表达业务目标。

例如，用户可以提出：

“帮我为这个新品类找到合适供应商，并准备一轮 RFx。”

寻源助手首先捕获用户意图，理解当前采购品类、预算、合规要求、供应商历史表现和业务优先级。随后，它会协调多个专门 Agent 分工处理。

供应商发现 Agent
基于品类、地区、风险、交付能力和历史绩效等维度识别潜在供应商，帮助采购团队更快形成候选供应商清单，并保留推荐理由。

RFx 编排 Agent
辅助生成或完善 RFx 内容，包括问题清单、报价结构、交付要求和评估标准，减少采购人员从零准备文档的工作量。

谈判智能 Agent
结合历史采购价格、市场趋势、供应商表现和合同条款，给出谈判策略和风险提示，帮助采购人员更有准备地进入谈判环节。

投标分析 Agent
对供应商响应进行比较，识别价格差异、条款风险、交付承诺和异常报价，辅助采购团队做出更清晰的决策。

在这些 Agent 之下，Skill 负责执行更具体、可控的系统动作，例如读取供应商主数据、检索合同条款、调用价格信息、创建 RFx 草稿、更新状态或触发审批流程。

这个例子体现了 Assistant 与 Agent 的核心价值：
用户不再被迫手工拆解流程、切换应用和串联任务，而是由 Assistant 理解业务目标，再由多个 Agent 协同完成复杂流程。

为什么 SAP Agent 不等同于通用 AI Agent

当前市场上有很多关于 AI Agent 的讨论，但 SAP 的 Agent 有非常明确的企业级边界。

在财务、采购、制造、销售和供应链等核心流程中，AI 不能只给出“看起来合理”的答案。它需要基于可信业务数据、标准流程、用户权限和可审计机制运行。

因此，SAP 的 Agent 能力强调以下几个基础：

业务流程基础
Agent 基于 SAP 长期沉淀的端到端业务流程和行业最佳实践，而不是孤立的任务自动化。

数据语义基础
Agent 需要理解企业业务对象、流程关系和上下文信息。SAP Business Data Cloud、SAP Knowledge Graph 等能力将为代理式 AI 提供更可信的数据和语义基础。

治理与权限基础
Agent 的执行需要遵循用户权限、业务规则、审计要求和合规边界。对于关键业务流程，human-in-control 仍然是重要设计原则。

云 ERP 与 clean core 基础
标准化流程、clean core 架构和持续创新机制，是客户持续吸收 SAP Business AI 创新的基础。

这也是 SAP Business AI 的差异化所在：AI 能力不是脱离系统运行的外部工具，而是嵌入在 SAP 应用、数据和流程治理体系中。

从 AI Feature 到端到端流程协同

过去，SAP Cloud ERP 中的 AI 创新更多体现在单点功能中，例如 AI 辅助错误解释、字段推荐、业务见解生成、文档信息提取或智能建议。这些能力已经显著提升了用户效率。

而 Assistant 与 Agent 的新范式，则进一步把 AI 能力组织到端到端流程中。

例如：

Source to Pay
支持寻源、RFx、合同、采购申请、供应商确认、发票校验和付款建议等流程环节。

Lead to Cash
支持线索识别、销售机会推进、报价、订单处理、开票、收款和争议处理等场景。

Record to Report
支持日记账、对账、差异分析、错误处理、关账、现金洞察和财务分析等任务。

Supply Chain Management
支持需求计划、生产计划、库存调整、物流执行、生产订单检查和异常响应等场景。

这意味着 AI 的价值不再只是提升某个操作步骤的效率，而是帮助企业缩短从业务信号到行动执行的时间。

对 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 客户的启示

对于 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 客户来说，Assistant 和 Agent 的落地并不是孤立的新功能启用，而是与云 ERP 标准化、业务数据质量和流程治理能力密切相关。

客户可以从以下几个方面提前准备：

识别高价值业务场景
优先关注高频、重复、跨步骤、数据充分且业务价值清晰的流程，例如采购寻源、订单异常处理、关账、现金管理、生产订单检查等。

明确自动化边界
区分哪些任务可以由 Agent 自动完成，哪些任务需要用户确认，哪些任务必须经过审批或审计。

完善数据和权限基础
Agent 的建议质量依赖于业务数据质量，执行能力依赖于清晰的权限模型和组织职责设计。

坚持 clean core 原则
越标准、越可升级的系统架构，越容易持续吸收 SAP 后续发布的 Business AI 创新。

用业务结果衡量价值
例如流程周期是否缩短，人工处理工作量是否下降，异常处理是否加快，合规风险是否降低。

获取最新 AI Feature 信息

SAP Business AI 相关能力更新较快，建议客户和合作伙伴定期访问 SAP Business AI Feature Catalog，按产品、业务领域和场景查看最新 AI Feature 信息：

https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/ai-catalog

同时，关于如何在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 中激活 SAP Business AI 功能，可参考 SAP Note 3651017。

总结

SAP 新一代 Assistant 与 Agent 的发布，标志着 SAP Business AI 正从单点辅助能力进一步走向面向业务流程的代理式 AI。

Assistant 负责理解用户意图和协调工作，Agent 负责完成专业任务，Skill 负责执行确定性动作。整个过程运行在 SAP 的业务数据、标准流程、权限和治理框架内。

对于 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 客户而言，这一方向并不是简单增加一个聊天入口，而是推动企业从应用驱动的操作方式，逐步走向以业务目标为中心的智能流程协同。

随着更多 Assistant、Agent 和 AI Feature 持续发布，SAP Cloud ERP 将进一步帮助企业构建更加智能、敏捷和可持续演进的运营核心。

entry.title: Re: Duplicate Serial Number while doing OBD - PGI
entry.content:
Hi Vamankumar,

Did you set the stock check to error in the serial number profile (transaction OIS2)? Then you'd get get an error message if you issue the same material/serial number combination a second time.

There's more info on this in the following note: https://me.sap.com/notes/612132

BR,
Alex

entry.title: Re: PFCG Role Generation After S/4HANA Upgrade: New T-Cod...
entry.content:
Will the role load only the new objects and fields related to the newly added T-code A, or will it also load the new objects and fields for all T-codes already assigned to the role?
When execute the update it will load (Add/delete/change) all need object, not only the new transaction that you added.
It is the same action as SU25 2C (Got role list), then merge in PFCG. In effect, the role will be synchronized with the current repository, which can introduce required objects, update existing entries, and remove items that are no longer aligned.

Best regards,
SAP Support
Xuejiao

entry.title: Re: 原価センタを無効化する手順と、無効にした際の影響について
entry.content:
Hello Sir,

To clarify — OKEON is for maintaining the Cost Center Standard Hierarchy, not for deactivating individual cost centers. For deactivation, please use KS02 and set the Valid To date.

Regarding the impact of changes made in OKEON, here is what you need to be aware of:

Reporting Any change in the hierarchy will take effect immediately and will also affect historical periods. All reports using the standard hierarchy (e.g., S_ALR_87013611, Fiori analytics) will reflect the new structure right away. Please inform your business users before proceeding.

Allocation Cycles If your assessment (KSV5) or distribution cycles (KSU5) reference the affected hierarchy nodes, moving cost centers between nodes may change your allocation results. Please review these before making any changes, especially near period-end.

Mid-Year Restrictions SAP will block organizational reassignments (company code, business area, profit center) on a cost center mid-year if:

  • Actual postings already exist in the current fiscal year
  • The cost center is linked to a Fixed Asset, Work Center, or HR master record

For further reference, please see the following SAP documentation:

  • Cost Centers and the Standard Hierarchy — SAP Help Portal
  • Deactivation of finance master data objects in S/4... - SAP Community

entry.title: Bridging the Gap: Connecting SAP Digital Manufacturing to Joule via SAP Build Studio
entry.content:
Enabling Joule for SAP Digital Manufacturing with an MCP Server

This article explains how to extend Joule with live SAP Digital Manufacturing context by exposing SAP DM APIs through a Model Context Protocol server deployed on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry.

Why Joule Needs SAP DM Connectivity

Joule is positioned as the AI copilot across SAP’s cloud suite, but it does not automatically understand what is happening on the shop floor unless SAP Digital Manufacturing data is made available to it. For example, if a user asks about today’s OEE, open non-conformances, delayed production orders, or work center performance, Joule needs access to live execution-layer data before it can answer accurately.

Without SAP DM connectivity, Joule lacks access to real-time production order status, OEE and performance data, quality inspection and non-conformance records, shift and crew context, material consumption, scrap, process parameters, and operator annotations.

In short, Joule can provide planning-layer intelligence, but it cannot reliably answer execution-layer questions without SAP DM connectivity. The implementation below bridges that gap by using an MCP server as the integration layer between Joule and SAP Digital Manufacturing.

Solution Overview

The solution uses an MCP server to wrap selected SAP Digital Manufacturing APIs and expose them as callable tools for Joule. Joule sends a user request to the MCP server, the MCP server calls SAP DM using service-bound credentials, and the response is returned to Joule with live shop floor context.

 

The high-level flow is: user question in Joule, tool selection by the agent, MCP tool execution, SAP DM API call, live SAP DM response, and final conversational answer from Joule.

What MCP Does in This Architecture

Model Context Protocol is an open protocol that allows AI assistants to discover and call external tools and data sources in a standardized way. Instead of building a custom integration for every AI client, the MCP server exposes business capabilities once and makes them available to any compatible AI client.

In this setup, the MCP server hosted on BTP CF on DM tenant, acts as a facade over SAP Digital Manufacturing APIs. It converts SAP DM capabilities into reusable tools that Joule can call during a conversation, such as retrieving SFCs for an order, checking quality records, or reading execution status.

Project Structure

sap_dm_mcp/

├── server.js          ← MCP server, tool definitions, Express endpoint

├── dm_client.js       ← SAP DM API client with token caching

├── btp_config.js      ← Config loader (VCAP_SERVICES + env vars)

├── .env.example       ← Local dev environment variable template

├── manifest.yml       ← BTP Cloud Foundry deployment config

├── xsuaa-config.json  ← XSUAA service OAuth configuration

└── Procfile           ← CF startup command

Configure SAP DM Service Binding and Credential Loading

1. Create the XSUAA Service

The MCP server first needs an XSUAA service instance so the Cloud Foundry application can support OAuth-based authentication.

cf create-service xsuaa application sap-dm-mcp-xsuaa -c xsuaa-config.json

This service is used by the MCP application runtime on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry and should be bound to the MCP server application.

2. Bind the SAP Digital Manufacturing Service

For SAP DM access, the MCP server reads the Digital Manufacturing service binding directly from Cloud Foundry instead of relying on a manually configured destination for the SAP DM API call.

In the SAP BTP cockpit, open the subaccount where SAP Digital Manufacturing is provisioned, go to the Digital Manufacturing service instance, create a binding for the MCP server application, and ensure that the binding exposes the SAP DM API endpoint and OAuth credentials.

3. Load Credentials from VCAP_SERVICES

When the application is deployed to Cloud Foundry, bound service credentials are injected into VCAP_SERVICES. The btp_config.js file reads the Digital Manufacturing service binding and extracts the SAP DM API base URL and OAuth token details. It is important to bind digital-manufacturing-service to MCP server app so that MCP is able to retrieve and utilize service credentials for api call. The destination services on xsuaa can allow only reading app destinations but here we need something similar to BTP destination.

//Load using
const vcap = process.env.VCAP_SERVICES;

//Read the service creds

const dmService = vcap["digital-manufacturing-services-quality"]?.[0];

config.dmBaseUrl = dmService?.credentials?.["public-api-endpoint"];

config.destination = {

  clientId: dmService?.credentials?.uaa?.clientid,

  clientSecret: dmService?.credentials?.uaa?.clientsecret,

  tokenUrl: dmService?.credentials?.uaa?.url + "/oauth/token"

};

This approach avoids hardcoding credentials and keeps the application portable across environments, provided the correct SAP DM service binding exists.

Implement Token Handling in dm_client.js

// Token cache

let _destToken, _destExpiresAt, _destBaseUrl;

async function fetchDestinationToken(config) {

  if (_destToken && Date.now() < _destExpiresAt - 30_000) {

    return { token: _destToken, baseUrl: _destBaseUrl };

  }

// Fetch fresh token from DM service binding's token URL

  const resp = await fetch(config.destination.tokenUrl, {

    method: "POST",

    headers: {

      Authorization: "Basic " + Buffer.from(

        `${config.destination.clientId}:${config.destination.clientSecret}`

      ).toString("base64"),

      "Content-Type": "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"

    },

    body: "grant_type=client_credentials"

  });

const data = await resp.json();

  _destToken = data.access_token;

  _destExpiresAt = Date.now() + data.expires_in * 1000;

  _destBaseUrl = config.dmBaseUrl;

  return { token: _destToken, baseUrl: _destBaseUrl };

}

The client caches the OAuth token and refreshes it only when required, using a 30-second buffer before expiry. This avoids unnecessary token requests while keeping API calls authenticated.

HTTP Basic Authentication is used for the token request, which handles special characters in the client secret cleanly compared with passing credentials through form encoding.

export async function dmGet(endpoint, params = {}) {

  const { token, baseUrl } = await resolveAuth(await loadConfig());

  const url = buildUrl(baseUrl, endpoint, params);

  const resp = await fetch(url, { headers: authHeaders(token) });

  return handleResponse(resp, endpoint);

}

Build the MCP Server and Register SAP DM Tools

The server.js file is the main application entry point. It creates an Express application, exposes a POST /mcp endpoint using Streamable HTTP transport, and registers SAP Digital Manufacturing tools that Joule can discover and call.

import express from "express";

import { McpServer } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/mcp.js";

import { StreamableHTTPServerTransport } from "@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/streamableHttp.js";

const app = express();

app.use(express.json());

app.post("/mcp", async (req, res) => {

  const transport = new StreamableHTTPServerTransport({ sessionIdGenerator: undefined });

  const server = createServer();

  await server.connect(transport);

  await transport.handleRequest(req, res, req.body);

});

app.get("/health", (_, res) => res.json({ status: "ok" }));

app.listen(process.env.PORT || 3000);

Deploy the MCP Server to SAP BTP Cloud Foundry

The manifest.yml file defines the Cloud Foundry application name, runtime, memory, health check endpoint, service bindings, and environment variables required for deployment.

applications:

  - name: sap-dm-mcp-server

    memory: 256M

    disk_quota: 512M

    instances: 1

    buildpacks:

      - nodejs_buildpack

    command: node server.js

    health-check-type: http

    health-check-http-endpoint: /health

    services:

      - sap-dm-mcp-xsuaa

      - sap-dm-destination

    env:

      DM_BASE_URL: https://api.test.region.dmc.cloud.sap

      DM_PLANT: PLANT1

      DM_USE_DESTINATION: "true"

      DM_DESTINATION_NAME: "EXE"

      NODE_ENV: production

After deployment, the bound services populate VCAP_SERVICES automatically. The application then reads the required SAP DM endpoint and OAuth credentials without manual credential entry.

Validate the Deployment

After pushing the application to Cloud Foundry, test the health endpoint using the generated application route with the /health suffix.

GET https://sap-dm-mcp-server.<your-cf-domain>/health

A successful deployment returns a response such as { "status": "ok" }. Once this is working, the MCP server is ready to be connected to Joule through SAP Build Studio.

Connect the MCP Server to Joule in SAP Build Studio

Create a new agent project in SAP Build Studio and add a Joule agent. Define the agent’s expertise, instructions, and supporting context prompts. During development, use a lower reasoning or thinking setting to control token usage, then adjust the model configuration before publishing for wider use.

Create a destination for the MCP server in the Joule subaccount, using no authentication. Add this destination in Studio Control Tower so Joule can communicate with the MCP server.

The destination is used by Joule to reach the MCP server. The MCP server itself uses the SAP DM service binding to authenticate against SAP Digital Manufacturing, so the Joule destination is not used for SAP DM authentication.

Release and Test the Joule Agent

To release the project, ensure that a shared environment exists. If you do not have permissions to create it, request support from an administrator before publishing the agent.

After release, go to Control Tower, open the assistant, and confirm that the new agent is available. Test it with a SAP DM-specific question, such as asking for the list of SFCs for a production order. If the configuration is correct, Joule should call the MCP tool, retrieve SAP DM data, and return a contextual response. Below are some tests I ran:
Prompt: Use my agent and get the data for production orders for Material AIRP_PURIFIER_FG and plant 4101. Analyse it considering the orders released in last 3 months and based on it forecast the orders which can be planned for next 6 months.

 

 

 

 

You can see that Joule now can retrieve production insights from execution and provide analysis/predictions. Hope you find value in this blog.

entry.title: Re: Stop Users from Typing Invalid Values in SAPUI5 ComboBox – A Practical Validation Approach
entry.content:
Hi Community,

"

A ComboBox is not automatically restricted to its items. If your requirement is to allow only predefined values, you must explicitly validate the user input before processing it.

The goal is not to remove the flexibility of sap.m.ComboBox, but to balance it—keep the search experience intact while ensuring only valid, business-approved values make it to the backend.

Kindly Note: We could have used a value help dialog for selection, but the requirement specifically called for a dropdown-based experience. That’s why the focus stayed on sap.m.ComboBox and a deeper validation approach around it.

"

 

Thanks for your selfless sharing, it really help us a lot.

🙂

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: Business Data Architecture: Modeling Layers for Structured Transformations
entry.content:
Transformations end up everywhere

In the previous post, we established platform responsibility: who owns data and business meaning. We saw four principles - Cross-Platform Access, Business Meaning Ownership, Controlled Reuse, and Single-Source Ingestion - that prevent platform silos, duplication waste, metric drift, and ownership confusion.

But platform ownership alone doesn't prevent transformation chaos. Even when teams know WHO owns a metric, they still need a shared understanding of which transformations have been applied to data - and which haven't.

Without modeling layers, teams cannot answer critical questions about the data they receive:

  • Has this data been harmonized, or is it still in source format?
  • Have business rules been applied, or do I need to implement them?
  • Are metrics already defined, or am I receiving raw transactions?
  • What quality and structure can I expect from this dataset?

This lack of shared understanding leads to two failure patterns:

  • Teams duplicate transformations because they don't trust what's already been done. A Snowflake team receives harmonized customer data from SAP Datasphere but reimplements the harmonization logic anyway - "just to be safe" - because there's no common language for understanding transformation state.
  • Teams skip transformations because they assume work that hasn't been done. A Power BI team receives data and assumes business logic has been applied, building reports on incorrect assumptions about metric definitions - leading to silent errors that only surface when reports are reconciled.

Without a shared structure for how transformations are organized, you cannot trust what you receive or know what still needs to happen.

This problem exists even within a single platform. A Snowflake-only environment with three teams - Finance, Sales, and Operations - each building their own reporting layers. Finance defines "Customer" during harmonization, Sales defines it in the semantic layer, Operations never defines it at all. When a dashboard pulls from all three, "Total Customers" shows three different numbers - same platform, same source data, three different transformation paths.

The problem scales dramatically across multi-platform landscapes. When SAP Datasphere structures transformations one way and Snowflake structures them differently, cross-platform integration becomes guesswork - but the root cause is the same: no shared understanding of transformation state.

Modeling layers provide structure

Modeling layers solve this by creating a shared structure for transformation organization. They define which kinds of transformations are allowed at each stage of the data flow. Each layer has a distinct responsibility - technical handling, domain alignment, business definition, or analytical interpretation - ensuring these concerns stay separated.

The concept of layered architecture isn't new. SAP BW has LSA++. Snowflake and Databricks have Medallion architecture (Bronze, Silver, Gold). What's different here is one modeling discipline that works consistently across all of them - whether you run analytics on a single platform or across SAP BW, SAP Datasphere, Snowflake, Databricks, Microsoft Fabric, and any other platform in the landscape.

The architecture defines five modeling layers, representing a one-way flow from source data to analytics:

 

This structure prevents transformations from being applied arbitrarily. Ingestion preserves source data, harmonization standardizes it, the definition layer establishes business meaning, the semantic layer applies analytical interpretation, and the sharing layer controls exposure - each transformation happening exactly once, in the right place.

Metrics and measures are defined before analysis. Analytical preparation supports evaluation without changing meaning. Final analytical logic and KPI evaluation are applied only where analysis is performed. This separation enables predictable impact, clear lineage, and safe reuse.

One discipline across all platforms

Each platform implements the five modeling layers using its own technical constructs - SAP Datasphere uses spaces, Snowflake uses schemas, Databricks uses Delta layers - yet all follow the same separation of concerns.

The same five logical layers map to different technical implementations:

 SAP DatasphereSnowflakeMS FabricDatabricks
IngestionInboundRawBronzeBronze
HarmonizationHarmonizationStagingSilverSilver
DefinitionPropagationIntermediaryGoldGold
SemanticReportingMartGoldGold
SharingOutboundSecure Views / Data SharingOneLake Shortcuts / Power BIUnity Catalog Shares

Note that Medallion architecture (Fabric, Databricks) combines business definition and analytical interpretation in the Gold layer. Where our five-layer model separates Definition (establishing business meaning) from Semantic (applying analytical logic), Medallion's Gold encompasses both - containing business-ready aggregates, star schemas, and semantic models together.

Cross-platform agreement

Beyond technical mapping, platform owners must explicitly agree on which transformations belong in which layer. Technical constructs vary by platform, but the responsibility assignments must be consistent - what counts as harmonization in SAP Datasphere must align with what counts as harmonization in Snowflake.

This isn't just about following a standard. It's about creating shared understanding across platform teams.

When a Snowflake team consumes data from SAP Datasphere's harmonization layer, they need to know exactly what transformations have been applied and which haven't. When they receive data from the definition layer, they must trust that business meaning has been established. Without this agreement, teams make assumptions about data quality and structure that lead to silent errors.

Cross-platform agreement is what allows teams to trust shared data.

When you receive data from the Definition layer, you know business meaning has been established - you can build analytical interpretations without redefining metrics. When you receive data from Harmonization, you know structures are standardized but business logic hasn't been applied yet - you understand exactly what still needs to happen.

This shared structure must be maintained as platforms evolve. When harmonization transformations in SAP Datasphere change, Snowflake teams consuming that data immediately understand what changed - because both platforms follow the same modeling layer discipline. The shared structure tells them: harmonization transformations were updated, business meaning in the definition layer remains unchanged. When new platforms are added to the landscape, they adopt the same layer discipline to maintain this shared understanding across the entire analytics landscape.

Controlled exposure through the sharing layer

 The Sharing layer deserves special attention because of one important characteristic: data can be exposed from any upstream layer - not just from the Semantic layer.

This flexibility is critical where different consumers need data at different stages of refinement - data scientists need harmonized transactions, cross-platform teams need defined metrics without analytical interpretation, and BI users need fully calculated models.

 

 The Sharing layer serves different types of consumers, each needing data at different stages of refinement. Consumer-specific shaping - such as column filtering, regional access controls, or format conversion - is applied without introducing new business logic:

  • Share from Ingestion layer: SAP Datasphere shares raw S/4HANA extracts to Snowflake's landing zone - original source structure preserved, with column filtering for sensitive fields
  • Share from Harmonization layer: Databricks shares harmonized IoT sensor data to SAP Datasphere for operational reporting - data is standardized and cleansed, with device-level access controls applied
  • Share from Definition layer: SAP Datasphere exposes the Revenue metric (defined once) to Snowflake for manufacturing profitability analysis - metric definition preserved with consumer-specific currency formatting
  • Share from Semantic layer: Snowflake shares analysis-ready revenue reports to Power BI - fully calculated models with row-level security for regional teams (EMEA users see only EMEA data)

Critical constraint: The Sharing layer applies security, access control, and formatting only. It must not introduce new business logic or analytical transformations.

This ensures that shared data preserves the meaning defined in upstream layers.

This implements Post 3's "Controlled Reuse" principle - data is shared across platforms without silent redefinition of business meaning.

Common concerns

When organizations first encounter modeling layers, several practical questions arise:

Does this take away team autonomy? No. Modeling layers do not control what teams build. They define where different types of logic belong, so teams can work independently with trusted data - knowing which transformations have already been applied and can be reused by others. Without this clarity, teams duplicate work because they don't know what exists or can't trust its quality.

Why can't we just transform data wherever it's convenient? Because convenience-driven transformations lead to duplicated calculations, inconsistent metrics and measures, and unpredictable change impact. When the same transformation appears in multiple places - some in ingestion, some in semantic models, some in reports - you cannot trace lineage, predict what will break, or trust that metrics mean the same thing everywhere.

Does this slow delivery? It may add a small amount of upfront discipline, but it reduces repeated downstream fixes by ensuring metrics and measures are defined before analytical interpretation - not recreated in every report or tool. Teams that skip the definition layer spend more time reconciling "why these two reports show different revenue" than they would have spent defining revenue once, correctly, upstream.

Do modeling layers force us into one technology? No. Modeling layers are a logical structure that can be mapped to different platforms while preserving the same business meaning and transformation discipline. SAP Datasphere, Snowflake, Databricks, and Microsoft Fabric all implement these layers differently - but the separation of concerns remains consistent.

Up next

Modeling layers define where transformations belong. The next post introduces how these transformations are organized into reusable, controlled assets:

Data Products as the Foundation for Controlled Reuse

  • How metrics, measures, and dimensions are packaged into data products
  • Why data products are the primary building blocks for analytics across platforms

entry.title: Upcoming Change: Member Limit Enforcement for Data Action Parameters
entry.content:
Overview

If you use data actions in SAP Analytics Cloud Planning, particularly those where story scripting is involved - whether triggering a data action directly or via a multi action - there is an important upcoming change you should be aware of.

With the Q4 2026 QRC release, we will be enforcing a hard limit on the number of dimension elements that can be passed via a parameter to a data action. This change is designed to help maintain system stability and ensure consistent, reliable execution across planning automations.

In this blog, we will walk you through 1) What the limit is and when it will be enforced, 2) How it may impact your existing data actions and scripting workflows, and 3) What you can do today if your use case requires passing a larger set of members

 

Dimension Parameter Member Limit for Data Action Scripting Objects

What’s Changing

From the Q4 2026 QRC release onwards, data actions will enforce a maximum limit of 1,000 member values per dimension parameter. This limit is most relevant in scenarios where story scripting is used to dynamically build and pass a member list to a data action parameter at runtime - for example, where a script collects a filtered set of members and applies them as a dimension parameter before triggering execution. This scenario also extends to cases where large parameter lists are passed to a multi action which in turn calls a data action and passes those parameters.

Note: This limit applies specifically to data actions. Multi actions themselves will not be immediately subject to the restriction (although that is likely to change in the future), but any data action called within a multi action will be subject to the 1,000 member parameter limit.

Early Warning: Toast Notifications

Ahead of the Q4 2026 QRC enforcement date, users will begin to see a toast notification when a data action is executed with a parameter that exceeds 1,000 members. The message will read as follows:

This early warning period gives you an opportunity to identify impacted data actions and make the necessary adjustments before enforcement begins. We strongly recommend treating these toast notifications as an action item - any data action triggering this message should be reviewed and updated ahead of the Q4 2026 QRC release.

Why We Are Making This Change

In some scenarios, very large static member list payloads passed to data action parameters have been seen to place significant strain on system resources. While such cases are not the norm, the potential impact on processing overhead means that introducing a clear upper boundary helps ensure stable and predictable execution for all users.

This limit acts as a practical safeguard - one that the vast majority of data action configurations will never encounter - while protecting the broader environment in cases where parameter payloads grow unexpectedly large.

Note: This change does not alter the business logic or behavior of your data actions in any way. It is purely a stability protection measure.

How to Check if You Are Affected

We recommend reviewing any data actions in your environment where story scripting is used to dynamically populate dimension parameter member lists before execution. Specifically, look for cases where a script collects or derives a member list and passes it to a data action parameter, and where the filter dimension involves a large dimension, potentially resulting a parameter volume exceeding 1,000 at runtime.  Additionally, check any scripted multi action executions that nest data actions where this also may occur.

Keep an eye out for the toast notification described above as this is the most direct signal that a given data action will be impacted by the enforcement change.

Recommended Approach: Use the Data Filter Parameter Type

If your use case genuinely requires filtering across more than 1,000 members, we recommend transitioning the data action script object to utilize the Data Filter parameter type, which is not subject to a member count limit (as it does not use a static dimension list).

The Data Filter parameter type provides a more scalable and flexible approach to scoping data action execution, allowing multiple filters to pass via a single parameter, and supporting input controls and filters using dynamic selection.  For more details on the data filter parameter please see: 

Improved Data Action scoping with the new Data Filter Parameter type

Summary

To recap the key points from this blog:

Topic

Detail

What’s changing

A member limit of 1,000 values per dimension parameter will be enforced for data actions

Enforcement date

Q4 2026 QRC release

Scope

Data actions, including those called by Multi Actions

Most commonly affected

Story scripting workflows that dynamically pass large member lists to data action parameters

Early warning

A toast notification will appear when the limit is exceeded ahead of full enforcement

When enforced

Execution will be blocked and a validation message is displayed

Why

To safeguard system stability in scenarios where very large static member list payloads place significant strain on system resources

What to do if you need more than 1,000 members

Transition to the Data Filter parameter type, which has no member count limit

Further reading

See our companion blog on the Data Filter parameter type (link above)

We appreciate your understanding as we work to ensure SAP Analytics Cloud Planning remains stable, reliable, and performant for all customers. If you have questions or need guidance on reviewing your existing data actions, please reach out through the standard support channels.

entry.title: AI Skills for SAP BTP – Part 1: Beyond Prompt Engineering
entry.content:
Why I Started Thinking About AI Skills

Like many SAP developers, I started using AI to make my daily work easier. Sometimes it helped me write a CDS model. Sometimes it explained an API that I wasn't familiar with. Sometimes it saved me from spending hours debugging an issue.

The more I used it, the more useful it became.

But after a while, I noticed something.

Most of my work wasn't about writing code.

It was about following a process.

When I deploy an application to SAP BTP, I don't just run a deployment command. I first check the target. I verify that I'm deploying to the correct space. I make sure the required plugins are installed. I confirm the deployment strategy. After deployment, I verify the application, check the services, and review the logs.

None of that is a single prompt. It's a workflow that experienced developers follow almost without thinking. That's when I started wondering whether AI should learn workflows instead of simply answering questions.

Prompts Are Good. Workflows Are Better.

A prompt is great when you want an answer to a question.

"Explain this error."

"Generate a CAP entity."

"Write a SQL query."

Those are all useful.

But enterprise development is different.

Most engineering tasks involve multiple steps. Some steps need validation. Some require confirmation. Some can be dangerous if executed incorrectly.

For example, deploying an application to the wrong Cloud Foundry space can affect other developers or even a production system.

In those situations, I don't want AI to simply give me the next command.

I want it to follow the same process that an experienced SAP developer would follow.

That's When the Idea of an AI Skill Came In

Instead of asking AI to answer a question, what if we asked it to perform a complete engineering task?

Not by taking control, but by guiding the developer through the entire workflow.

That's how I started thinking about AI Skills.

An AI Skill has a single purpose.

It knows what it is responsible for.

It follows a defined workflow.

It validates before acting.

It explains what it's doing.

It helps the developer make the right decision instead of simply generating commands.

In other words, an AI Skill behaves more like an experienced teammate than a search engine.

The Principles I Want Every Skill to Follow

This is the most important section in the article.

I would make it a simple list.

1. One Skill. One Responsibility.

Every skill should solve one problem well.

A deployment skill should focus on deployment.

A project validator should validate projects.

A troubleshooting skill should troubleshoot.

Keeping skills focused makes them easier to use and easier to maintain.

2. Follow a Workflow

Enterprise development is a sequence of steps.

AI should guide developers through those steps instead of jumping directly to the final answer.

3. Safety First

Some operations are harmless.

Others can change systems.

Some are irreversible.

Every skill should understand the difference and behave accordingly.

4. Explain Every Decision

Developers should always understand why a recommendation is being made.

AI should never feel like a black box.

5. Stay Within Scope

A deployment skill should not suddenly become a security advisor.

A HANA skill should not review UI code.

Every skill should have clear boundaries.

This Is the Journey I'd Like to Share

This article is the beginning of a series called AI Skills for SAP BTP.

Over the next few weeks, I'll be sharing the skills I'm building, the design decisions behind them, and the lessons I learn along the way.

The goal isn't to replace developers.

The goal is to capture good engineering practices and make them reusable.Some of the skills I'll be covering in this series include:

 

🚀 Deployment Skills

  • Cloud Foundry Deployment Assistant – Guides developers through a safe and structured deployment process with validations, confirmations, deployment verification, and troubleshooting.

✅ Project Quality Skills

  • CAP Project Validator – Reviews SAP CAP projects and identifies configuration issues, missing dependencies, and common development mistakes before deployment.
  • SAP BTP Compliance Analyzer – Checks applications against SAP BTP recommendations, security practices, deployment readiness, and platform configuration.
  • SAP BTP Best Practices Analyzer – Reviews projects against SAP development standards, coding guidelines, naming conventions, and recommended design patterns.

🔍 Troubleshooting Skills

  • Deployment Troubleshooter – Helps developers understand deployment failures, identify the root cause, and recommend possible fixes.

As the series grows, I'll also explore skills for other areas such as SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Integration Suite, SAP Fiori, and other common SAP BTP development tasks.

I hope these articles are useful whether you're just starting with SAP BTP or you're an experienced developer looking for ideas on how AI can support day-to-day work.

entry.title: Re: SAP Document AI Workspace: Features You Need to Know
entry.content:
@venkatamandavilli  :

Exactly — SAP Document AI Workspace acts as the central place to configure, manage, and monitor document processing end to end.

It allows teams to define document types, configure extraction and processing rules, review low-confidence results through human validation, and monitor processing performance and outcomes.

In practice, this helps reduce manual effort for documents like invoices, purchase orders, and other business documents while improving accuracy and handling exceptions in a controlled way.

entry.title: Project created from template - Company code not inherited from template (2)
entry.content:
PS project is created from template. Due to the integration with PPM, project template is created as operational project with CJ20N (but not CJ91).

The PS template project used is a multi company project structure (WBS assigned to different company codes within the WBS structure).

When creating an operational project in CJ20N by copy of template project, and changing the project profile, the company code of the template WBS is not copied into the target project, but instead, the company code is inherited from the project definition.  

On the other hand, the profit center is properly inherited from the PS project template. Therefore, the project cannot be saved because of discrepancy betwen profit center inherited from template and company code not inherited from template, but from project definition.

entry.title: Re: Duplicate Serial Number while doing OBD - PGI
entry.content: With the standard customizing content, you can't receive a material/serial number combination twice. If you'd do so, you'd get a status error, since you can't receive a serial number in status ESTO. In private cloud/OP, you can however change the system status customizing to allow this. You can check this in transaction BS22 => system status I0184/ESTO ==> business transaction PMS1/Goods Receipts.

entry.title: Re: Recommendations for Wrapper Interface Design
entry.content:
Great blog!

Could you please elaborate on some common challenges customers face in projects with wrappers and share some advice:
Who do you recommand to create Wrappers, the people wanting to use the SAP object for the first time? Or some central Team?
How do we make sure the Wrapper gets locked in the proper Transports and is available before depending objects get transported? Who is responsible for the wrapper once something changes in SAP? All users? A central commitee?

If I use the Compatibility of Released API checks, the wrapper needs to be kept stable, but if I use ABAP Cloud I need to create the contract early, to be able to use the Wrapper in ABAP Cloud, which prevents me then from changing it afterwards.
How do I solve that target conflict?

"Define smaller structures: Avoid tight coupling to SAP structures. Rather than reusing or copying large SAP structures, declare smaller, dedicated structures with required fields only. This reduces the dependency footprint."
How do we deal with the challenge of custom fields? If no SAP structures are included, custom fields will not come into the wrapper interface automatically. Even if we add them manually, each custom field then might lead to lots of wrappers needed to be adjusted and transported, etc.
Also don't dedicated structures defeat the purpose of reusable wrappers? Or would you recommend wrapping the same BAPI multiple times for different use-cases?

Thanks for the support.

entry.title: Re: DMEE Payment file format
entry.content:
ISO 20022 – pain.001.001.09: Key Structural Changes and Impact

The pain.001.001.09 message version introduces several structural modifications to the XML schema compared to previous versions (e.g., 001.03 or 001.06), including:

  • New tags and deprecation of obsolete elements.
  • Hierarchical changes (e.g., InstrPrty, SvcLvl, PmtTpInf).
  • Stricter validation rules for fields such as IBAN/BIC formats, dates, and amounts.
  • New fields related to traceability, regulatory reporting, and cross-border payments.

Impact on SAP Systems:
Internal systems for XML generation and parsing must be updated to ensure compliance, including:

  • Mapping adjustments (XSD schemas, field transformations).
  • Enhanced validation logic (unit and integration testing).
  • Testing and adaptation of existing workflows.

Start with KBA 3159759 - CGI: CGI_XML_CT, CGI_XML_DD Format Updates #38 (or more recent ones when available)

entry.title: Re: Automatically transfer text from the purchase order i...
entry.content:
Dear @dnikishaev_1,

The PO item short text is not transferred to the FI document item text by default during goods receipt this is standard behaviour, confirmed in SAP KBA 2921484 (PO short text in Goods Receipt not transferred to FI). However, SAP provides a standard no-development way to make this automatic. In the Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document app enter the PO, go to item level, open General Information -> Settings and activate Default Setting for item Short text. This is a one-time, per-user setting once set the PO item short text (EKPO-TXZ01) is automatically copied into the journal entry line item text (SGTXT) on every subsequent goods receipt for that user

If you need this independently of the per-user setting e.g. for MIGO/Web GUI or API-driven postings or applied for all users you can implement it via released extensibility BAdI FIN_CODING_BLOCK_SUBSTITUTION (Coding Block Substitution) reading the short text from released CDS view I_PurchaseOrderItemAPI01 and populating SGTXT

More details below:

SAP KBA 2921484 – PO short text in Goods Receipt not transferred to FI
SAP Help – Business Add-Ins for Substitution for Journal Entries
SAP Help – Purchase Order Item / I_PurchaseOrderItemAPI01

So the requirement is achievable in Public Cloud first try the standard Default Setting for item Short text user parameter, only if that does not cover your scenario, fall back to the released substitution BAdI

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: SAP Business One Down Payment Reconciliation Error
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing an issue in SAP Business One regarding internal reconciliation between A/P Invoices and a Vendor Down Payment.

Scenario:

  • SAP Business One Version: 10.0
  • Three A/P Invoices were posted on 10-May, 21-May, and 25-May.
  • The total invoice value is approximately ₹66 Lakhs.
  • All three A/P Invoices were posted with Withholding Tax (TDS).
  • Later, the company created a Vendor Down Payment Request for approximately ₹1.14 Crores and made the payment through Outgoing Payment by selecting the Down Payment Request.
  • Now the vendor has requested that the previous A/P Invoices be adjusted/reconciled against this Down Payment.

Issue:
When attempting to perform the internal reconciliation, SAP Business One displays the following error:

You can only link to the section of the document which has no withholding tax.

It appears that SAP does not allow reconciliation because the A/P Invoices contain Withholding Tax, whereas the Down Payment was paid without Withholding Tax.

Questions:

  1. Is this standard SAP Business One behavior?
  2. Is there any supported method to reconcile these documents without cancelling and recreating them?
  3. Are there any SAP Notes or best practices addressing this scenario?
  4. Has anyone encountered a similar issue, and if so, what was the resolution?

Any guidance or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Triggering SAP_COM_0682 Service
entry.content:
Hello,

System triggers this service when there is data in the information container. Document items should be marked before the payment run. So, the system can add document details to the information container with the payment run. To do so, below steps should be followed;

  1. "104798 - Make Settings for Information Container Categories" configuration step should be configured for "0003 - Payment Run" and "05 - SOAP API".
  2. Create a communication arrangement for "SAP_COM_0682" communication scenario.
  3. To add related FI-CA document items to the information container, each document item's, "Payment Info (DFKKOP-INFPA)" field should be filled with value "01 - Outbound Call of Web API". This field marks the item for information container.
  4. Schedule the payment run job from the "F3529 - Payment Run / Debit Memo Run" application. When job has completed successfully, system adds automatically the marked document item details to the information container. Added data could be displayed from the "FPINFCO2 - Display Information Container" application.
  5. A job should be scheduled from the "F5792  - Forward Data from Information Container" application to trigger outbound SOAP service of the SAP_COM_0682 service.

Kind regards,
Berke Günenç

entry.title: Intent Based Navigation with appSpecificRoute
entry.content:
Hi Colleagues,

We are developing navigation out of a Joule Capability, using intent based navigation. Our goal is to target an application with a specified object open already, ie, internal app navigation.

The target URL would be such as:
#IBPApplicationLog-display&/LogDetails/9FB4E95040ED6EF01900EADA8D2895A4

However, the documentation of Card Navigation Actions or navigation action handling do not mention anything about the app specific route parameter handling.

If i check the Navigation services API, i can easily achieve the goal by doing the following in the console:
oNavService = sap.ushell.Container.getService("Navigation"); 
...

oNavService .navigate({
target: {
semanticObject: "IBPApplicationLog",
action: "display",
},
appSpecificRoute: "LogDetails/9FB4E95040ED6EF01900EADA8D2895A4"
})
 
But we aren't sure how to pass such parameters, since the documentation only describes ibnTarget and ibnParams. Is there any description that tells us the syntax necessary to achieve our target url navigation?
 
Thank You and Best Regards,
Mihaly

entry.title: Deploy a SAP Fiori Elements App from BAS to S/4HANA Cloud and Register It as a Launchpad Tile
entry.content:
Most tutorials about deploying Fiori apps from Business Application Studio (BAS) stop at 'Deployment Successful.' But deployment is only the midpoint. For a user to actually find and launch your app in the SAP Fiori Launchpad, you need to wire up four more things: a crossNavigation inbound in the manifest, an IAM App, a Business Catalog, and a Launchpad Space. Get any one of these wrong and you hit silent failures — the app exists in the system but never shows as a tile.

Audience

This guide targets developers building SAP Fiori Elements applications in SAP Business Application Studio for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using OData V4 services.

 

Prerequisites

  • SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) subaccount with SAP Business Application Studio subscription
  • S/4HANA Cloud system with an active OData V4 service
  • A BTP Destination configured for your S/4HANA system
  • Eclipse ADT connected to your S/4HANA system

 

Architecture Overview

OData V4 Service (S/4HANA ABAP)
        │
        ▼
Fiori Elements List Report App  ◄── Generated in SAP BAS
        │
   npm run deploy  (deploy-to-abap, ui5-deploy.yaml, gCTS transport)
        │
        ▼
BSP Application + LADI /NAMESPACE/MYAPP_UI5R  (auto-created on deploy)
        │
        ▼
IAM App (External App type)  ◄── Created in ADT, Published Locally
        │
        ▼
Business Catalog  ◄── Created in ADT, Published Locally
        │
        ▼
Business Role  ◄── Assigned in Maintain Business Roles
        │
        ▼
Launchpad Space / Page  ◄── Tile visible to user

Step 1 — Open Business Application Studio via BTP Cockpit

BAS is accessed through the SAP BTP Cockpit — not through the S/4HANA Fiori Launchpad. Open BTP Cockpit → Subaccount → Instances and Subscriptions.

Find SAP Business Application Studio → click Go to Application. You land in the SAP Build Lobby.

 

Step 2 — Create the Project in SAP Build

In the SAP Build Lobby, click Create (top right) to launch the Create Project wizard.

Wizard Step 1 — Objective: Application

Wizard Step 2 — Category: Full-Stack

Wizard Step 3 — Type: Full-Stack Node.JS

Wizard Step 4 — Name and Dev Space

Provide a project name (e.g. myapp-audit-log) and create or select a Full-Stack dev space.

Click Review then Create. SAP Build provisions the dev space and scaffolds the project. 

 

Step 3 — Generate the Fiori App

With the project open in BAS, press Ctrl+Shift+P and run: Fiori: Open Application Generator

Template Selection — List Report Page

Data Source — Connect to a System

  • Data Source: Connect to a System
  • System: Your S/4HANA destination (e.g. GIE_080 (S4HC))

Once connected, choose your OData V4 service.

Entity Selection

  • Main Entity: Root CDS entity exposed by the service
  • Automatically add table columns: Yes
  • Table Type: Responsive

Click Finish. BAS generates and opens the project.

The SAP Fiori Launchpad discovers launchable applications through inbound navigation definitions stored in the application descriptor (manifest.json). During deployment, these inbounds are converted into Launchpad App Descriptor Items (LADI). Without them, the application can be deployed successfully but cannot be exposed through the Launchpad.

 

Step 4 — Add the crossNavigation Inbound to manifest.json

⚠️  This is where most guides go wrong by omission.

By default the Fiori generator creates an application-type app with no crossNavigation block. If you deploy as-is, the BAS terminal shows:

warn  No LADI was created as no inbound is defined in manifest.json
warn  App descriptor of type 'application' does not define a launchable inbound

Without a Launchpad App Descriptor Item (LADI), you cannot create an IAM App that references your app, and therefore cannot add a tile to a Business Catalog.

Fix: Open webapp/manifest.json and add a crossNavigation block inside sap.app:

"sap.app": {
  "id": "com.mycompany.myauditlog",
  "type": "application",
  "crossNavigation": {
    "inbounds": {
      "MyAuditLog-display": {
        "semanticObject": "MyAuditLog",
        "action": "display",
        "title": "{{flpTitle}}",
        "subTitle": "{{flpSubtitle}}",
        "signature": {
          "parameters": {},
          "additionalParameters": "allowed"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

Also add the keys to webapp/i18n/i18n.properties:

flpTitle=My Audit Log App
flpSubtitle=View sales order change history

📝  Tip on additionalParameters: Setting it to "" produces a harmless warning. Use the explicit string "allowed" or "ignored" to avoid it.

Step 5 — Create a gCTS Transport Request in ADT

The deploy-to-abap task requires a gCTS transport — a Workbench Request with target 1GT. A normal transport fails with:

Request does not have transport target 1GT

In ADT: Transport Organizer → right-click your user node → Create → Transport Request:

  • Type: Workbench Request
  • Target: 1GT

Note the transport number (e.g. GIEK9xxxxx) — you will plug it into ui5-deploy.yaml.

 

Step 6 — Configure ui5-deploy.yaml

Create ui5-deploy.yaml in the project root:

specVersion: "4.0"
metadata:
  name: com.mycompany.myauditlog
type: application
builder:
  resources:
    excludes:
      - /test/**
      - /localService/**
  customTasks:
    - name: deploy-to-abap
      afterTask: generateCachebusterInfo
      configuration:
        target:
          destination: GIE_080
          authenticationType: reentranceTicket
        app:
          name: /MYNAMESPACE/MYAUDITLOG
          description: My Audit Log App
          package: /MYNAMESPACE/MY_PACKAGE
          transport: GIEK9xxxxx
        exclude:
          - /test/
          - /localService/

⚠️  app.name must use your registered ABAP namespace prefix (e.g. /MYNAMESPACE/). Using a Z* name in a namespaced package causes: Customer object WAPA cannot be assigned to package

Step 7 — Build and Deploy

Open a terminal in the project root and run:

npm install
npm run build
npm run deploy

A successful deployment shows:

Inbound MyAuditLog-display was converted to LADI /MYNAMESPACE/MYAUDITLOG_UI5R
Launchpad App Descriptor Item /MYNAMESPACE/MYAUDITLOG_UI5R was created
SAPUI5 Application has been uploaded and registered successfully
Deployment Successful.

📝  Note the LADI ID (/MYNAMESPACE/MYAUDITLOG_UI5R) — you need it for the IAM App in the next step.

A Launchpad App Descriptor Item (LADI) is generated during deployment from the crossNavigation section of the application descriptor. It contains the metadata that allows the SAP Fiori Launchpad to discover the application.

 

Step 8 — Create the IAM App in ADT

In ADT: right-click your package → New → Other ABAP Repository Object → search for IAM App.

Fill in:

  • Name: /MYNAMESPACE/MYAUDITLOG_APP
  • Description: My Audit Log Fiori App
  • App Type: External App  ← critical (see gotcha below)
  • Fiori Launchpad App Descr Item ID: /MYNAMESPACE/MYAUDITLOG_UI5R

Select your transport request and click Finish.

Click Publish Locally.

⚠️  Do NOT select 'Fiori App' or 'UI Adaptation App' as the type. The UI5R descriptor created by deploy-to-abap is only compatible with External App. Wrong type gives:
    UI5R can only be used in Adaptation UI Apps

📝  Publishing auto-generates _UI5A and _UI5_EXT runtime artifacts. Always reference the IAM App by its logical name /MYNAMESPACE/MYAUDITLOG_APP, not the runtime objects.

 

Step 9 — Create and Publish the Business Catalog

In ADT: right-click your package → New → Other ABAP Repository Object → Business Catalog.

 

Name it (e.g. /MYNAMESPACE/MYAUDITLOG_BC), select your transport, click Finish.

In the Apps tab, click Add and search for your logical IAM App name.

 

 

Save, then click Publish Locally.

⚠️  The catalog MUST be Published before it appears in:
  • The Maintain Business Roles app's Business Catalog picker
  • The Select Catalogs dialog in the Launchpad page editor
If you can see a colleague's catalog but not yours, come back and verify the Publication State.

 

Step 10 — Assign the Catalog to a Business Role

In the Fiori Launchpad, open the Maintain Business Roles app:

  • Open your role → Edit
  • Business Catalogs tab → Add → search for /MYNAMESPACE/MYAUDITLOG_BC → OK
  • Set Access Category to Unrestricted
  • Save

⚠️  The Select Catalogs dialog in the page editor (next step) only lists catalogs already assigned to the role that owns the space. Skip this step and your catalog won't appear in the dialog even if it's published.

 

Step 11 — Add the Tile to a Launchpad Space

In Maintain Business Roles → your role → Launchpad Spaces tab:

  1. Click Add → Create New Space

  1. Open the space/page → Edit → click Select Catalogs

  1. Tick your catalog → Select
  2. On the Manually Selected tab, drag the tile into the page
  3. Add a Section Title, then Save.

 

Step 12 — Access the App

Hard-refresh the Fiori Launchpad (Ctrl+Shift+R). Navigate to your space.


 

Troubleshooting Cheat Sheet

Symptom

Root Cause

Fix

warn No LADI was created

No crossNavigation.inbounds in manifest.json

Add the crossNavigation block before deploying

Request does not have transport target 1GT

Wrong transport type

Create a gCTS transport (target 1GT) in ADT

Customer object WAPA cannot be assigned to package

Z* app name in a namespaced package

Use namespaced app name: /MYNAMESPACE/MYAPP

UI5R can only be used in Adaptation UI Apps

Wrong IAM App type

Create IAM App with type External App

My catalog missing from Select Catalogs

Not published or not assigned to the role

Publish in ADT + assign to Business Role + reload

403 Forbidden on BSP URL

UCON blocks direct access

Access via the Launchpad tile, not the direct URL

 

Summary

Although deploying a SAPUI5 application from SAP Business Application Studio is straightforward, making it available to end users requires several additional configuration steps within SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Each artifact—crossNavigation, LADI, IAM App, Business Catalog, Business Role, and Launchpad Space—depends on the previous one. Understanding this dependency chain makes troubleshooting much easier when an application deploys successfully but does not appear in the SAP Fiori Launchpad.

Tested on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP Build / BAS (2024/2025 release), @SAP-ux/ui5-deploy task via specVersion: '4.0' ui5-deploy.yaml.

entry.title: SAP Business One – Set Attachment Browse Dialog to Open Last Browsed Folder Per User
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a query regarding the Attachment functionality in SAP Business One.

Our client uses attachments extensively, with more than 50 users working in SAP Business One. Previously, when users clicked the Browse button in the Attachment window, the file dialog automatically opened the last folder that particular user had browsed, which made attaching documents very convenient.

However, after a recent change, the Browse dialog now always opens the standard/default folder instead of the user's last browsed location.

Since each user stores documents in different folders, they now have to navigate to their respective folders every time they attach a file, which affects productivity.

Is there any standard SAP Business One setting, configuration, or parameter that allows the Browse dialog to remember and open the last browsed folder for each user?

We are looking for a standard solution without any customization, if possible.

Any suggestions or guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One AddOnIdentifier property distribution
entry.content:
Hi,
Yes, with an Implementation Add-On Identifier you'll need to recompile for every client. That identifier gets generated on the customer's own network, using their implementation license, so it's tied specifically to that network and won't run anywhere else. So if you've got multiple clients, you're repeating that whole generate-and-recompile process for each one. Not great if you're planning to distribute this more broadly.
What you actually want for distribution is a Solution Add-On Identifier. To get one, you first need to request a BASIS license from SAP, by raising a message under the SBO-SDK-AA component, giving them your partner number and the solution's name, and letting them know whether it's for one specific customer or meant to be sold to multiple. Once SAP issues the BASIS license, you generate the Solution Identifier, embed it in your AddOnIdentifier property in code, and compile once. That same build can then ship to all your clients without any further recompiling.
On the three types you asked about:
Development is just for your own dev and testing while you're building the addon, not for handing off to customers.
Implementation is generated per client using their license, and you do need to recompile each time.
Solution needs a BASIS license from SAP up front, but once you have it, one compiled addon works across all your clients.

So bottom line, if you're shipping this to multiple customers, go with Solution instead of Implementation.

entry.title: Re: Help regarding SAP Business One integration
entry.content:
Hi,

You're using the wrong tool for what you're building. The Extension Registration Data Generator (.ard) is for the new lightweight/browser client, not for classic COM-based UI automation, that's why it shows incompatible in the regular client and only shows up under lightweight extensions in the browser. For your case, skip that tool entirely. Just register and run your exe as a classic add-on via Administration, Add-ons, Add-on Manager in the regular fat client.

On connection: when SAP launches your exe as a registered add-on, it passes you a connection string via command line, you read that and call SboGuiApi.Connect with that string. That's the proper production way. The fixed debug connection string you see in SDK samples only works for attaching to an already-running client during development and testing, SAP doesn't recommend using it in production.
AddonIdentifier is unrelated to connecting, it's just for SAP's license service to recognize your add-on as a distinct product. You set it after connecting, not before.
So bottom line: drop the lightweight extension approach, build and register as a classic add-on, use the SAP-provided connection string at runtime, and set AddonIdentifier once you're past pure dev and testing.

entry.title: Re: Error KOB1
entry.content:
Hi Serena,

Thank you for sharing the details.

For this kind of issue, could you kindly create a support case with the relevant attachment and reproduction steps? We may need to check your specific scenario in more detail, including the background execution, export/download method, system release, and the data selection used in KOB1.

This will help us better understand why the number of rows in the Excel output differs from the result shown in the system.

Kind regards,
